     fresh competitive/citation landscape · shipped-dist audit), 2026-06-01. Web-verified where flagged.

# Sugi Atlas — How to be *uniquely* attractive to AI/LLMs

This is a fresh, deeper pass than `01`–`04`. Those docs nailed the on-page mechanics
(JSON-LD, llms.txt, declarative leads, provenance, content depth, the cross-entity
graph). This pass deliberately goes around them to answer the question the brief asks:
**given that biobtree's MCP server is *already* used by OpenAI/Claude/Gemini clients,
where can Sugi Atlas win AI attention that no competitor can copy — and where is the
prior research's advice actually *wrong*?**

It draws on four parallel analyses, each web-verified or live-probed against the public
biobtree REST (`https://sugi.bio/biobtree/api/map?i=<id>&m=<chain>`). Full drafts:
[`_drafts/A_ai_mcp_ecosystem.md`](_drafts/A_ai_mcp_ecosystem.md),
[`_drafts/B_synthesis_moats.md`](_drafts/B_synthesis_moats.md),
[`_drafts/C_competitive_citation_landscape.md`](_drafts/C_competitive_citation_landscape.md),
[`_drafts/D_dist_audit.md`](_drafts/D_dist_audit.md).

---

## ⚠️ Reconciliation — decisions & fixes since this draft (2026-06-01, later same day)

This draft was written against an earlier dist and an LLM-summary assumption that no
longer hold. The strategic core (§1–§4, §7–§9) is unaffected and stands; but before
acting on **§5/§6**, note:

- **No data-dump sidecars, by design.** `bundle.json` and `provenance.json` were
  *deliberately dropped* — each entity ships only `page.md` + `entity.jsonld`, with dataset
  provenance in frontmatter (`datasets:`) and a planned `/methods` page. So §5.1 is **not**
  "ship the missing sidecars" — it's that the draft `llms.txt` over-promises artifacts we
  chose not to publish. **`claims.jsonld` is a *different* artifact** (atomic citation
  records, not a raw dump) and remains worth building (M3).
- **LLM summaries are disabled (dev-stage); `llms.txt` is deprioritized.** There is no
  `## Summary` block to label — the top-of-page slot is filled deterministically
  (declarative lead + **At a glance** digest + NCBI Overview). This *inverts* §5.2: the
  honesty posture is now **stronger** ("zero LLM-authored facts"), not a risk. Per project
  decision, `llms.txt` stays as cheap infra but **is not a priority** — don't let its stale
  promises drive page/sidecar decisions, and don't spend time polishing it now.
- **Shipped since the draft:** deterministic **At a glance** digest (gene/disease/drug — a
  down-payment on §4's "computed verdict" moats); **Related-molecules** dual-source
  (ChEMBL + PubChem) enrichment; **decorative per-row link removal** (kept JSON-LD `sameAs`
  — exactly §1.2's "JSON-LD for the graph, not citation"); thin-section **merges** on drug
  pages; **patent** dominant-structure quantification + filed biobtree gaps **#25/#26/#27**.
- **§6 polish pass shipped this session** — see the `[FIXED 06-01]` tags inline. The
  structural items (cross-entity mesh) remain open and are the next big workstream.

---

## 0. The one-paragraph thesis

Sugi Atlas's single un-copyable asset is that it lives on the **same host as a live
biomedical MCP server that AI agents already call** (`sugi.bio/biobtree/mcp`), and that
server's paper is literally titled *"Grounding LLM Responses with Large-Scale Structured
Biomedical Data."* No reference competitor (NCBI, UniProt, Open Targets, GeneCards,
Pharos) controls **both** the query tool **and** a citable human page. The winning move
is not "make pages more AI-friendly" — that lever is commoditizing and partly mythical
(see §2) — it is to weld the MCP/API and the static pages into a single **grounding
flywheel**: an agent queries biobtree → biobtree hands back the matching **citable Atlas
URL + claim IDs** → that URL surfaces as the source in ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity → the
citation pulls more agents to the MCP server. The MCP server is the *acquisition
channel*; the static page is the *citation artifact*; the biobtree provenance chain is
the *trust substrate*. Everything below either builds that loop, fills it with content
no single source can compute, or removes a launch blocker that breaks it.

---

## 1. Three findings that reframe the whole plan

1. **The competition moved from "pages" to "MCP servers."** Since the last landscape
   pass (two weeks ago): **Open Targets shipped an *official* MCP server**, **DisGeNET
   shipped "AIDA," a natural-language AI assistant** (v26.x), and a third-party MCP
   ecosystem (BioMCP, BioContextAI) now fronts UniProt/Ensembl/HPA/Reactome/AlphaFold/
   openFDA/ClinicalTrials. **But almost all of them return data with *no canonical
   human-readable URL to cite*.** That is the gap Atlas fills — and Sugi is the only
   one that owns both the server and the page. *(Agent C)*

2. **The "JSON-LD gets you cited" thesis is empirically false.** A controlled 2026
   Ahrefs study (1,885 pages, add JSON-LD vs control) found **no citation lift** —
   Google AI Overviews actually **−4.6%** — and a separate test showed engines read the
   **rendered HTML, not the hidden markup**, at retrieval time. The strongest *causal*
   evidence (Princeton/GA-Tech/AllenAI GEO study, KDD-2024, 10k queries) says what
   actually moves citations is **quotable statistics (+32%), expert/source quotations
   (+41%), and explicit primary-source citations (+30%)** — all content, no markup.
   `01_landscape_and_ai.md` ranked "embed schema.org JSON-LD" as the **#1 highest-leverage
   move for AI citations**; that ranking is wrong. Keep JSON-LD — but re-justify it for
   *agent/MCP parsing, the cross-entity knowledge graph, and Google **Dataset** Search* —
   not as a citation lever. *(Agents A + C, independently.)*

3. **Citations concentrate, and the path in is the graph, not merit.** Wikipedia is
   ~26–48% of ChatGPT's top citations; every major engine grounds on Wikidata; for
   *medical* queries Perplexity prefers NIH/PubMed/clinical-DB sources and Google AI Mode
   applies a **binary YMYL trust gate** (fail it → not cited at all; 44.1% of medical
   queries trigger an AI Overview — 2× baseline, so the prize is large). An unknown static
   site does not get cited on merit; it gets cited by being **seeded into the graph**
   (Wikidata/Wikipedia references, dataset registries) and **retrieved live** by an agent
   that already had a reason to fetch it. The MCP server is that reason. *(Agents A + C.)*

---

## 2. Myths to stop believing (carry-overs from 01–04 to correct)

| Belief in 01–04 | 2026 evidence | What to do instead |
|---|---|---|
| schema.org JSON-LD is the #1 AI-citation lever | Ahrefs controlled test: no lift, AIO −4.6%; engines parse rendered HTML | Keep JSON-LD for the **cross-entity graph + Dataset Search + agent parsing**; stop budgeting it as a citation lever |
| llms.txt is a Path-A priority for AI-friendliness | ~10% adoption; **408 of 500M** AI-bot hits; Google ignores it; added noise to a citation model | **Deprioritized by project decision (2026-06-01).** Demote to "infra, like a sitemap"; keep the cheap draft but don't invest, and don't let its stale promises drive page/sidecar decisions |
| `ask.md` as FAQPage schema | Google **removed FAQ rich results 7 May 2026** | Keep `ask.md` **as content** (direct-answer pages); do **not** type it `FAQPage` |
| Classic SEO top-10 ranking ⇒ AI citation | Only ~38% of AI citations are top-10 organic; organic↔AI overlap fell 70%→<20% | Optimize for **extractable answer structure + trust**, not rank |
| Declarative-lead prose is the differentiator | Everyone now writes "answer-first" leads | The durable edge is the **uncopyable computed fact** + the MCP channel, not prose polish |

Do **not** mass-seed Wikipedia/Wikidata or manufacture mentions — spam systems block
exactly what AI relies on. Seed only where Atlas is genuinely the best source for a
*specific computed fact*. Do **not** chase Reddit (the #1 AI-cited domain) — it is the
citation surface for opinions, not for a ClinVar count; Atlas's lane is the
primary-source-grounded tier. *(Agents A + C.)*

---

## 3. The strategy — the grounding flywheel (NEW; the headline)

These are the moves no competitor can run, ordered by leverage. Tag: **biobtree-side**
(MCP/API change), **atlas-side** (generator/page change), or **distribution**.

### M1 — biobtree MCP tool results return the citable Atlas URL + claim IDs `[NEW, biobtree-side]`
When the MCP server answers a gene/drug/disease query for an entity that has an Atlas
page, append a structured pointer to the result: `atlas_page:
https://sugi.bio/atlas/gene/TP53/`, `atlas_claims: …/claims.jsonld`, and a one-line
"cite this page" hint. The server already resolves the entity ID, so this is cheap.
**This is the flywheel's ignition** — it converts *existing* MCP traffic into Atlas
citations at near-zero acquisition cost, and no one else can do it because no one else
owns both halves. *Effort: med.*

### M2 — Expose Atlas as MCP **Resources** + ship a `ground_and_cite` MCP **Prompt** `[NEW, biobtree-side]`
Add two under-used MCP primitives to the biobtree server: (a) **Resources** serving
`atlas://gene/TP53` → the page's `claims.jsonld`/markdown so an agent can read the frozen
artifact through MCP, not just HTTP; (b) a **Prompt** template that teaches the agent
"answer from biobtree data, then cite the matching `sugi.bio/atlas/...` page and its
claim IDs." Teaching citation behavior **once, server-side** means every connected client
(Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex) inherits it. No biomedical resource ships an MCP prompt
that teaches grounding+citation. *Effort: med.*

### M3 — `claims.jsonld` as the atomic RAG-ingest unit, with content-addressed fact IDs `[reinforces 04, re-prioritized to a hard #1 artifact]`
One record per assertion: `{subject_id, predicate, object_id, evidence_level,
source_dataset, source_url, biobtree_chain, claim_id}`, where `claim_id` is a content
hash of the normalized triple (stable across regenerations unless the fact changes).
2026 citation-enforced biomedical RAG consumes exactly this — pre-chunked,
evidence-leveled, provenance-stamped records with a stable handle. It is also the payload
M1/M2 hand back, and the join target for every §4 synthesis. *Effort: high (the join is
the work; rendering is trivial).*

### M4 — Seed Wikidata first, Wikipedia references second `[NEW, distribution]`
Highest-probability path to *actual* citations, and 01–04 never mention it. (a) Add a
`described at URL` statement on each entity's existing Wikidata item pointing at its Atlas
page; (b) where a Wikipedia article cites a database for a fact Atlas computes uniquely
(a concordance count, a CIViC verdict), add Atlas as the reference — **honestly, only
where it's genuinely the best source.** Wikidata feeds every major AI's grounding graph;
entity signals propagate in ~4–6 weeks. *Effort: med, ongoing; start with the ~30
reference entities.* **Caveat: editors revert self-promotion; no bulk spam.**

### M5 — Register the corpus as a schema.org **Dataset** in the registries `[NEW, distribution]`
Target the RAG *supply chain*, not just answer engines: (a) ship a `Dataset`/`DataCatalog`
JSON-LD (distribution = the JSON sidecars; license; `creator`; `version` = biobtree
release; `temporalCoverage`) for **Google Dataset Search**; (b) mirror `claims.jsonld`/
`bundle.json` as a **HuggingFace dataset** with a card linking to `sugi.bio/atlas` (HF
datasets are themselves indexed by Dataset Search and are where RAG builders shop); (c)
apply for a **Bioregistry** prefix + **FAIRsharing** record. Every download is a potential
downstream citer, and life-sciences-native registry listing buys credibility no GEO tactic
can. *Effort: med.*

### M6 — List the biobtree MCP server in the registries; position it as "biomedical grounding" `[NEW, distribution]`
Publish in the **official MCP Registry**, **mcp.so**, **glama.ai**, **PulseMCP**,
**Smithery**, **awesome-mcp-servers** — each listing stating the server returns citable
`sugi.bio/atlas` pages. The "biomedical / RAG-grounding" category is near-empty; this is a
land-grab that feeds M1's channel. *Effort: low (metadata submissions).*

### M7 — Get the biobtree v2 preprint to cite `sugi.bio/atlas` + deposit a Zenodo DOI `[NEW, distribution]`
The paper is forthcoming. One paragraph + URL naming Atlas as biobtree's human-readable,
citable rendering, plus a DOI'd corpus snapshot, gives Atlas a peer-reviewed,
PubMed-adjacent anchor — the strongest possible E-E-A-T signal for medical AEO, free.
*Effort: low.*

### M8 — "Frozen MCP answer" pages: render the exact biobtree query + a "run live" link `[NEW, atlas-side]`
The provenance already stores the chain that produced each section. Render it as a
copy-pasteable MCP/REST recipe (`>>hgnc>>clinvar>>mondo>>mesh`) with a "run this live" link
to `sugi.bio/biobtree/api/...`. The page becomes a *cached, re-executable grounding query*,
not a dead document — the strongest reproducibility/E-E-A-T proof ("don't trust us, re-run
it"), and no competitor exposes the exact query behind each fact next to a live endpoint.
*Effort: med (data already in provenance).*

### M9 — Named, credentialed authorship + machine-checkable methods + medical disclaimer `[reinforces, sharpened by YMYL evidence]`
For YMYL, the **#1 cited-medical-source signal is named credentialed authors with linkable
profiles**, and E-E-A-T correlation roughly triples on medical queries. Put an
ORCID-linked maintainer + a "no fact on this page is LLM-authored; the one synthesis block
is labeled" methods statement in the page + `Dataset` JSON-LD `author`/`creator`, plus a
"reference use, not medical advice" footer. Turns the "LLM-summarized → AI slop" perception
risk into a best-in-class transparency story. *Effort: low.*

### M10 — A thin public `atlas_lookup` / `atlas_concordance` MCP tool `[NEW, biobtree-side]`
Beyond the general graph tool, a tool that takes an entity and returns the answer-first
lead + claim IDs + citable URL in one call — and `atlas_concordance` returning the
AlphaMissense×SpliceAI×ClinVar shortlist. "An answer engine in a tool": the agent gets a
citable, pre-synthesized answer it can quote directly. *Effort: med (over `claims.jsonld`).*

---

## 4. Content moats — computable facts no single source can produce (NEW)

These passed a hard test: **(a)** join ≥2 datasets no competitor co-locates, **(b)** produce
a crisp citable statement, **(c)** are deterministic from biobtree. All were **live-probed**
against the public REST (row counts are real). They are *additive* to 04's already-planned
syntheses (AM×SpliceAI×ClinVar concordance, druggability A–E tier, DepMap/ClinGen verdict,
cross-entity hubs). Each one is also an atomic `claims.jsonld` record (M3). Full detail +
example output sentences in [`_drafts/B_synthesis_moats.md`](_drafts/B_synthesis_moats.md).

| Rank | Moat (the citable answer) | The join | Effort | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Variant pleiotropy fan** — "rs1042522 drives 11 GWAS traits across 4 domains, with p-values" | `rsid >> dbsnp >> gwas (>> efo)` | L | data already on §6/§12; client-side group-by |
| 2 | **Allele→drug→disease→direction CIViC matrix** — signed sensitivity vs resistance tally per gene/drug | `gene >> hgnc >> civic_evidence` (**100 rows, 1-hop**) | L | **NEXT.md marks this "route TBD" — now confirmed working.** Unblocks the variant tier cheaply |
| 3 | **Undrugged-essential gap index** — "selective dependency + has structure + driver + zero phase-≥1 drug" | `depmap (pct_dependent + common_essential) × chembl phase × pdb/alphafold × intogen` | L | DepMap **confirmed live & discriminating** (MTOR 98.3%/essential vs TP53 0.4%); the `common_essential` flag stops false positives |
| 4 | **Tissue-restricted target safety flag** — on-target toxicity verdict | `bgee expression_breadth × depmap essentiality × chembl phase` | L | `expression_breadth` enum is a free schema field no one exploits |
| 5 | **Drug off-target selectivity index** — "85 measured targets; selectivity window N log-units; liability tissue X" | `chembl_molecule × chembl_target × chembl_activity pChEMBL × bgee` | M | imatinib = 85 targets confirmed; the drug-page analog of the gene druggability verdict |
| 6 | **Cohort pathway convergence** — "the computed molecular convergence point of disease X's gene cohort" | `cohort.fan() × reactome is_disease_pathway × msigdb` | M | label as "convergence count," not p-value (no background universe) |
| 7 | **Signed mechanistic regulator card** — unified up/down protein+TF+chemical regulators | `signor (signed) × collectri × ctd` | M | everyone else's STRING/IntAct is *unsigned*; this is *directed* |
| 8 | **miRNA-specificity hub** — "dedicated vs shotgun" miRNA regulators ranked by genome-wide promiscuity | `mirdb target_count` ratio | L | zero new fetches; data already in §9 |
| 9 | **Inheritance-vs-somatic dual-role classifier** — "germline AD Noonan ↔ somatic oncogene, same mechanism" | `gencc × intogen role × clingen_dosage` | M | the RASopathy↔cancer sentence no single source assembles |
| 10 | **Pharmacogene actionability density** — "tier-1 pharmacogene; top drugs by annotation weight" | `pharmgkb clinical/variant/pathway counts` | L | counts are prebuilt in schema; PGx depth no one renders as a verdict |
| 11 | **"Hot on every axis" residue rank** — "KRAS G12C: AM 1.00 + ClinVar Path + CIViC Level-A sotorasib" | AM × spliceai × clinvar × civic_evidence allele | M | apex synthesis; needs HGVS normalization (the real work). Sequence after #2 + 04's concordance |
| 12 | **Paralog-redundancy escape flag** — *why* a driver is only selectively essential | `depmap × paralog` | M | hypothesis-generating; state the caveat |

**Verified-blocked / don't build yet:** `brenda`/`gtopdb` from uniprot returned 0 rows on
the public instance (contradicts 02's positive result — reconfirm the route);
`chembl_mechanism`/`drug_indication` return no schema (drug MoA free-text route still TBD —
use ChEBI definition + trials instead); deep ortholog conservation is only ~4 rows
(Compara not ingested). *(Agent B.)*

The compounding point: facts 1, 2, 3, 4, 9, 10 each emit **one atomic, ID-joined,
evidence-stamped triple**. Sequencing them *into* `claims.jsonld` (M3), not just into body
prose, is where the RAG-citation moat compounds — an engine can lift "KRAS G12C →
sotorasib → NSCLC → CIViC-A" as a provenance-stamped record no competitor publishes.

---

## 5. The real Phase-0 — make the published contract match shipped reality

Agent D's audit flagged "missing sidecars" and "unlabeled LLM prose" as launch blockers.
Both were **resolved by decision, not by building more** — see the Reconciliation note.
The actual Phase-0 is narrower:

1. **Sidecar contract — the only artifact to *add* is `claims.jsonld`.** Each entity ships
   `page.md` + `entity.jsonld` *by design*; `bundle.json`/`provenance.json` were dropped
   (provenance lives in frontmatter `datasets:` + a planned `/methods` page). So there is
   no "404 workflow" to fix by resurrecting dumps — the fix is to **stop the stale
   `llms.txt` draft from promising them** (and llms.txt is deprioritized anyway). The one
   artifact still worth building is **`claims.jsonld`** (M3) — atomic, content-addressed
   citation records, *not* a raw bundle — because it's the payload M1/M2 hand back and the
   join target for §4. Until it ships, M1 should point at the page URL + section anchor,
   not a claims file.
2. **The honesty story is already won — keep it deterministic.** There is no `## Summary`
   LLM block to label: LLM summaries are disabled and the top-of-page slot is deterministic
   (declarative lead + **At a glance** + NCBI Overview). This is *better* than §1.3/M9 asked
   for — "zero LLM-authored facts on the page" is the strongest YMYL transparency posture.
   Phase-0 action is only to **state this explicitly** (a one-line "no fact here is
   LLM-authored" methods note, → M9), not to retrofit a disclosure onto an LLM block that
   doesn't exist.
3. **Page polish — mostly shipped this session** (see §6 `[FIXED 06-01]`). Remaining gating
   item before a bot-facing launch is the **cross-entity mesh** (§6 top two items) — the
   next big workstream, not a quick fix.

NEXT.md's warning against crawling an incomplete corpus still holds — but "incomplete" now
means "before the cross-entity graph + `claims.jsonld`," not "before the data dumps."

---

## 6. High-value page defects that suppress citation (from the live audit)

Verified on shipped files; `[FIXED]` since 04, `[STILL]` broken, `[NEW]` first flagged here.
Severity is "how much it hurts LLM ingestion/citation." Full list + `file:line` in
[`_drafts/D_dist_audit.md`](_drafts/D_dist_audit.md).

- **`[NEW]` Gene JSON-LD has zero cross-entity edges** — drug→gene and disease→gene edges
  exist, but **gene→disease and gene→drug do not**, despite the body carrying GenCC/
  ClinGen/CIViC/ChEMBL data. The graph is asymmetric; an agent can't traverse from
  `TP53.jsonld` to Li-Fraumeni or its drugs. *(High; medium effort.)*
- **`[NEW]` Every cross-entity edge URL leaks offsite, never to an Atlas page** —
  sotorasib's `target` KRAS points to genenames.org even though `gene/KRAS/` exists. **There
  is no internal Atlas↔Atlas mesh** in JSON-LD or body links. This is the single biggest
  *unrealized* asset (04's "self-closing graph") and it's still wide open. *(High; medium.)*
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` Run-on duplicated subcellular-location string** — `_dedup_sentences`
  collapses the repeated period-separated segments in the UniProt subcellular-location CC
  ("Cytoplasm. Nucleus. PML body. …" now once each).
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` Mangled/empty MeSH rows** — gene §12 now drops nameless descriptors
  (`| C538339 | | |` gone); MeSH/EFO/etc. IDs also de-linked to plain text in the
  consistency pass.
- **`[NEW/STILL]` Disease readable lead buried under inline JSON-LD** — the ~50-object
  `associatedGene` array still renders before the lead. With `bundle.json` dropped, the fix
  is to **trim/cap the inline JSON-LD `associatedGene` array** (full set → the future
  `claims.jsonld`/methods page), not move it to a bundle. *(Med-high; low.)*
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` "VIP entries" mislabel** — the upstream `is_vip` flag is degenerate
  (true for every gene record), so it's **dropped**; disease PGx now reports only PharmGKB
  coverage + CPIC dosing-guideline counts.
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` Unrounded float artifacts** — shared `fnum()` rounds pAffinity / pChEMBL
  / value / dependency-% display values (no more `6.170000076293945`).
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` Dangling "PharmGKB curates …:" → nothing** — the colon only renders when
  counts follow; the zero case gets a clean "no annotations" note.
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` Ampersand headers** — section headers now use "and" (`Drug and
  pharmacology data`), so anchors are clean (`#drug-and-pharmacology-data`).
- **`[STILL]` 300–504-row inline PDB tables** bloat context and push the citable
  Disease-associations anchor far down on TP53. Cap + `<details>` (theme/web-dev lane) or
  move the long tail to the future `claims.jsonld`. *(Med; low.)*
- **`[PARTIAL 06-01]` Drug mechanism prose** — ChEBI roles + definition now surface in the
  merged `## Drug identity and classification` §1; a dedicated `## Mechanism` is still
  optional. *(Low-med; low.)*
- **`[FIXED 06-01]` `biobtree_version`** — relabelled `"…-dataset integration"` (a dataset
  fingerprint, not a fake version); `generated_at` remains the replay anchor. A real version
  needs a biobtree `/api/meta` version field (small upstream ask, not filed yet).

Genuinely **fixed earlier** (banked): disease declarative leads, graceful 0-GWAS note,
hedged PCD tractability reframing, CIViC verdict in the lead, named isoforms present.

---

## 7. White space to own (ranked) — queries with no good citable page today

1. **Long-tail "[gene] [variant] [drug] response" precision-oncology verdict pages** — zero
   competition; CIViC triples (CC0) already in hand; exactly what Perplexity/AI-Mode answer
   and exactly the +41%/+30% GEO levers. (Moat #2 + #11 feed these.)
2. **Per-gene druggability/tractability A–E verdict pages** — Pharos gives only a TDL token,
   Open Targets buries it in GraphQL; no one publishes a citable one-line answer. (Moat #3.)
3. **Cross-dataset concordance facts** — "high-confidence damaging variants in BRCA1" is
   *uncomputable* on any single source (04's AM×SpliceAI×ClinVar; Moat #11).
4. **Free, no-auth disease-cohort syntheses with clinical framing** — owns the long tail
   MalaCards/DisGeNET wall off and Open Targets hides in a SPA. (Moat #6, #9.)
5. **The "citable home for MCP/API answers" + cross-entity hub pages** ("drugs targeting
   KRAS", "genes in disease X") — MyGene/Open-Targets-MCP/UniProt hand agents data with no
   URL to footnote; Atlas's page + co-located MCP closes the loop no one else can.

---

## 8. Sequenced plan

**Phase 0 — mostly done (2026-06-01).** The page-polish items (run-on subcellular string,
MeSH rows, float rounding, dangling PharmGKB colon, VIP mislabel, ampersand anchors,
`biobtree_version`) **shipped this session** (§6 `[FIXED 06-01]`). The contract items
resolved by decision (§5): no data dumps to ship; the honesty story is already
deterministic. **Remaining Phase-0 before any bot-facing launch:** trim/cap the inline
disease JSON-LD `associatedGene` array (§6), add the one-line "no LLM-authored facts"
methods note (M9), and — the real gate — the cross-entity mesh + `claims.jsonld` (which is
Phase 2). llms.txt is deprioritized; don't block on it.

**Phase 1 — the cheap, uncopyable content (days–1 wk).** Build the low-effort Moats #1, #2,
#3, #4, #8, #10 (§4) — each is a client-side rule over data already on the page. Promote the
CIViC signed verdict and the gap-index/safety verdict into the lead and into `claims.jsonld`.

**Phase 2 — close the graph + ship `claims.jsonld` (1–2 wk).** Add gene→disease/gene→drug
JSON-LD edges; point every cross-entity edge whose target has an Atlas page at the **internal**
URL; render "Related Atlas pages" blocks; emit `claims.jsonld` with content-addressed IDs
(M3). This is the highest *unrealized* structural asset (§6, M3).

**Phase 3 — the flywheel + distribution (2–4 wk).** biobtree returns Atlas URLs/claim IDs in
MCP tool results (M1); Atlas Resources + `ground_and_cite` Prompt (M2); the thin
`atlas_lookup`/`atlas_concordance` tool (M10); "run-live" query recipes on pages (M8).
In parallel (no code): register the MCP server in the directories (M6), the corpus as a
schema.org Dataset + HuggingFace + Bioregistry/FAIRsharing (M5), seed Wikidata (M4), wire the
preprint citation + Zenodo DOI (M7), add named authorship + methods + disclaimer (M9).

**Phase 4 — scale + harvest.** Build Moats #5, #6, #7, #9, #11 (medium effort); synthetic
hub + variant×drug long-tail pages (§7); after deploy, watch AI-fetch patterns and which
pages get cited, and feed that back into the lead-sentence shapes.

---

## 9. The single most important sentence

If only one thing ships beyond Phase 0: **make the biobtree MCP server hand back the citable
Atlas URL + claim IDs in its tool results (M1), backed by a real `claims.jsonld` (M3).** That
is the one move that turns biobtree's *existing* OpenAI/Claude/Gemini traffic into Atlas
citations, and it is the one move no competitor in the world is positioned to copy.
